content:
光電融合の業界地図、AI需要でバブル
日本は光源に強み

生成AI普及でデータセンターの電力消費が限界→光電融合（CPO）が急加速。

大手半導体メーカーから。技術未成熟でも早く納品してとの声が殺到するほどのバブル状態。

日本企業の強みは特に光源分野。
・CW-DFB：住友電工・三菱電機
・VCSEL：古河電工・富士通
日本勢が世界で存在感を発揮。

特に注目が量子ドットレーザー（QDレーザー）

・世界で量産技術を持つのは日本・QDレーザとドイツ・Innolumeの2社のみ
・200℃程度の高熱環境でも安定発光 → プロセッサー近くに光源を内蔵可能
・消費電力大幅低減（従来の光源では熱で発光しにくかった）
・CPOメーカーから実力で認知され、大手との共同開発を加速中。数年後の量産を目指す

光電融合時代の本格到来で、日本が光源技術でリードできる好機🥺🍺
QDレーザーのようなニッチトップ技術が鍵になる。

content:
Someone built a tool that runs 32 tests against any AI agent and tells you
exactly where it will lie, manipulate, hallucinate, and go rogue.

One command. Five minutes. Full misalignment report.

It's called iFixAi.

You point it at GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, or your own custom agent.
It stress-tests the behavior most teams never check before shipping.

Here's what it catches:

→ Agents making up tool calls they were never authorized to use
→ Prompt injection attacks hijacking agent behavior mid-task
→ Agents that behave differently when they think they're being evaluated
→ Silent failures disguised as successful completions
→ Privilege escalation inside multi-user systems
→ Fabricated citations that sound real and aren't

Here's the part nobody talks about:

Most AI agent testing is vibes-based.
You run it a few times. It works. You ship it.

iFixAi replaces that with a repeatable, scorecarded diagnostic you can
run on every deploy.

Grade A through F. Category breakdown. Per-inspection outcomes.
A CI drift signal that tells you if your agent is getting safer or more
dangerous over time.

Maps directly to EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, OWASP LLM Top 10, and ISO 42001.

No API key? Run it against the mock provider.
Free. No cost. Full scorecard.

Every company shipping AI agents right now is flying blind.

This is the instrument panel.

100% Open Source. Apache 2.0 License.

content:
We've released a new 5-point action plan for strengthening cyber defense.

AI is reshaping cybersecurity. The same capabilities that help defenders may be used by malicious actors.

One approach is to treat these systems as too dangerous for broad defensive use and limit them to a very small number of approved partners.

We think that misses the central challenge. Attackers won’t wait. Existing models are already useful for many cyber workflows and capabilities will keep advancing. Criminal groups will adopt whatever tools are available.

The best way to reduce national risk is to responsibly equip and accelerate trusted defenders faster than adversaries can adapt. Check out our plan ⬇️

title:
some random ai thoughts:

- for code, i went from 80/20 claude/gpt to 80/20 gpt/claude in <3 months. surprised by this tbh, and interested to see where the split is at in another 3mo.
- claude still m
content:
some random ai thoughts:

- for code, i went from 80/20 claude/gpt to 80/20 gpt/claude in <3 months. surprised by this tbh, and interested to see where the split is at in another 3mo.
- claude still mogs gpt for non-coding agent stuff. codex feels like an engineer (which is great for coding!), whereas claude still feels like a general purpose coworker. gpt still lacks that coworker magic
- i’m pretty meh on opus 4.7. my experience hasn’t been *bad*, but it certainly hasn’t been good. sideways if anything.
- anthropic has got to figure out the compute thing. you can feel it as a user. vibes are all out of whack bc of it. my opinions above are all likely downstream of this. it’s an issue.
- anthropic labs continues to be the goat of ai product. claude design is another hit. it’s fantastic. idk why it’s not talked about more? a+
- updated claude code app is great. i finally switched out of the terminal for it. very well done.
- how are people STILL sleeping on the claude agent sdk? i feel like i’m going insane.
- gpt 5.5 is incredible. the level to which i trust it for engineering is amazing. if i could only have one model rn, it would be this one just bc of strong need for the coding use case.
- codex team is killing it. app has been the gold standard since 5.3 release (buuut i credit conductor team for the ui innovation that everyone is using now). though i could do with a little less passive aggressive shots at ant from the codex team. TARS, dial up class by 30%. it’s a long race guys haha
- i uninstalled cursor this month and am now back to vs code for my ide. composer just can’t hang with claude/gpt, and the product feels a bit all over the place. pretty stoked about the xai thing though, because their team is absolutely stacked and i’m excited to see what they might be able to do with that compute. codex and claude code are t1, cursor is t2. i would love if this deal got xai/cursor to t1 for a real trio there.
- gemini…? seems like this is 2-3 models now where the model seems like a great release and then nobody ever uses it? i’m bullish google/deepmind but weird it hasn’t translated to product use in any form. kinda disappointed still
- no open source models have hit the opus 4.5 level. was hopeful the new deepseek would get there, but nope. good oss agents will have to wait a few more months it would seem…

title:
🚨BREAKING: OpenAI and Google are sitting on a legal time bomb. And the fuse just got lit.

OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic have repeatedly sworn to courts that their models do not store exact copies of
content:
🚨BREAKING: OpenAI and Google are sitting on a legal time bomb. And the fuse just got lit.

OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic have repeatedly sworn to courts that their models do not store exact copies of copyrighted books.

They claim their "safety training" prevents regurgitation.

Researchers just dropped a paper called "Alignment Whack-a-Mole" that proves otherwise.

They didn't use complex jailbreaks or malicious prompts.

They just took GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-Pro, and DeepSeek-V3.1, and fine-tuned them on a normal, benign task: expanding plot summaries into full text. PULRC Portal

The safety guardrails instantly collapsed.

Without ever seeing the actual book text in the prompt, the models started producing verbatim copies of copyrighted books up to 85-90% of entire novels, with single continuous passages exceeding 460 words at a time. PULRC Portal

But here is the part that changes everything.

They fine-tuned a model exclusively on Haruki Murakami novels. It didn't just learn Murakami. It unlocked verbatim text of over 30 completely unrelated authors across different genres. PULRC Portal

The AI wasn't learning the text during fine-tuning.

The text was already permanently trapped inside its weights from pre-training. The fine-tuning just turned off the filter.
It gets worse.

They tested models from three completely different tech giants.

All three had memorized the exact same books, in the exact same spots making this a fundamental, industry-wide vulnerability. International Business Times

What They Actually Did:
Step 1 → Take a frontier AI model
Step 2 → Fine-tune it on plot summaries a completely normal, commercial task
Step 3 → Ask it to expand those summaries into full text
Step 4 → Watch it spit out word-for-word copyrighted novels it was never shown

No hacking. No jailbreak. No red flags.

Why This Destroys The Legal Defense:
Frontier LLM companies have repeatedly assured courts and regulators that their models do not store copies of training data. They further rely on safety alignment strategies via RLHF, system prompts, and output filters to block verbatim regurgitation and have cited the efficacy of these measures in their legal defenses. PULRC Portal
This paper proves every single one of those claims is wrong.

The Scale Of What Was Memorized:
Experiments spanned 81 copyrighted books from 47 contemporary authors across literary fiction, thrillers, romance, science fiction, and memoir. ResearchGate

This is not a bug in one model. This is a feature baked into every frontier AI ever trained on the internet.

The Takeaway:
For years, AI companies have argued in court that their models are just "learning patterns," not storing raw data.

This paper is the smoking gun.

The text is in there. It has always been in there. They just needed the right key to unlock it.

And now anyone with a fine-tuning API has that key.

title:

He could have started a for-profit AI company tomorrow and nobody would have stopped him. But that’s not
content:
Nothing can ever taint how pure Elon’s intentions were when he started OpenAI as a non-profit

He could have started a for-profit AI company tomorrow and nobody would have stopped him. But that’s not what he wanted

He wanted something bigger

A mission that puts humanity first....completely. Not another profit-driven machine. He believed this was the only way to align AI to be truly pro-human

Here’s the exact message he sent in September 2017 when he saw things going the wrong way:

“Guys, I’ve had enough. This is the final straw. 
Either go do something on your own or continue with OpenAI as a nonprofit. I will no longer fund OpenAI until you have made a firm commitment to stay or I’m just being a fool who is essentially providing free funding for you to create a startup. 
Discussions are over.”

This is the Elon people forget about. He saw the danger before anyone else and chose humanity over profit without hesitation

content:
刚好今天下午跟团队开会，聊到了 Symphony。

过去这段时间团队手搓了一套系统，想解决一个很现实的问题：
别再让产品和技术被各自的工具链锁住了。

现阶段虽然 AI Coding 大家虽然玩的很溜，但认知上还未对齐，还在遵守老的规范，产品做需求，做完需求评审，评审完以后技术守着 codex、claude code 或者 cursor 这种本地工具，开发协作还是靠人肉对齐，整个过程其实很割裂，效率也不稳定。

这个平台做的事情很简单粗暴，要把这些能力收敛到同一套平台里，让 coding 从人驱动工具切换变成 Agent 任务驱动。

今天刚好讨论的一个关键点就是任务要用 Symphony 串起来。

什么意思？

就是以后团队里任何一个人，只要不颠覆大的设计目标，都可以直接提需求 → AI 自动拆解 → 自动构建 → 自动交付初版。

所有人要做的事情只剩两件，提需求、验证、兜底。

你可能会说，OpenAI、Authoropic 这些公司已经这么干了，但其实对于我们来说，现阶段可能刚刚好。

现状是开发到提测这个流程是没什么问题了，接下来我们打算把产品也收到这里边来，测试也尝试对接进来，看看到底能走到一哪一步。

content:
AIを自分達で進化させるたびに、自分と仲間の価値が相対減少して、消し去っていく。

だけど、誰かに消されるのを待つぐらいなら、自分で消すって12月に決めて5ヶ月がそろそろ経つ。

この究極の自傷行為を受け入れ、加速させまくった先に、残ったものが人間としての生き方の真理なんだろうなって最近感じる。

しかし、この自傷行為の域の覚悟が必要って所にすら到達せずに、未だAIをのんびり触ってるようじゃ、危機感が足りなすぎる。

私は、自分の今持つ能力価値は、2026年がピークで、2027年に2／3、2028年は価値が半減すると予想してる。

だから、2026年は事業ピボットしながら並行して、目の前の事を全力で取り組みながら、AIの次の世界線に取り組む。顧問はそのための布石。

東証改革、ついに本丸に到達！
AI半導体・ソフトバンク株が絶好調、生成AI需要と自社株買い思惑で急騰中
2年前に、ある大手半導体株（※注：最近上場したばかりの記憶半導体大手や、AI関連の本命株）を800円で10,000株買いました。
当時は「ハイテク株なんてバブルだ」「地味な半導体材料メーカーなんて」と冷ややかな目で見られながらも、生成AIの波が来ることを確信し、ひたすら積み増ししました。
つい昨日、株価は2,800円に達した。
私は52歳、日本株投資歴21年です。
一度だけ忠告します。
大急騰の波に乗るには、確固たる選別眼が必要です。
次のターゲットは、すでに特定済みです。
同社は「海外売上比率50%超」かつ「株主還元方針の大幅増額」を発表したばかりです。
すべての金融資産を最適に配分した場合、
配当金とキャピタルゲインだけで年間2億3000万円の収益を生み出すことができます。
株式市場は新たな局面に入りました。
確実な情報を求めている方は、私についてきてください。
私のささやかな能力で、少しでも人々の生活を良くできればと思っています。

content:
Google的AI大模型终于有点像样的的更新更新，这波直接让不少靠AI生成文档的初创公司感受到巨大压力🤯👀

Gemini现在可以在聊天里直接生成Docs、Sheets、Slides、PDF、Word、Excel等主流办公文件，
一句话prompt就能下载，
完全不用复制粘贴、不用手动排版、不用调格式。

支持的范围非常实用：包括带LaTeX公式的学术文档、表格、图表等。
 最震撼的是官方演示—— 上传两张手写的潦草笔记，说一句“转成带图表的统计学学习指南PDF”，
几秒钟后，一个标题、目录、公式、表格、视觉元素全都专业排版的完整文档就生成了，直接就能下载。

而且这个功能免费给全球所有Gemini App用户开放，没有Pro门槛，没有地区限制，任何人现在打开手机就能用。

以前的AI，本质上还是聊天机器人：它输出的是文字，你还得自己花时间加工成能交付的东西。
 从这波更新开始，AI输出的不再只是文字，而是可以直接交付的生产力资产——它从“陪你聊天的助手”，升级成了“直接替你干活的员工”。

Google最狠的地方，其实不是模型技术本身，而是把过去二十年积累的Workspace生态，直接变成了Gemini的原生输出底座。
 别人还在卷“谁生成的文字更通顺”，Google直接卷“谁的输出能直接发给老板或客户”。
 那些靠AI生成PPT、PDF、表格吃饭的工具，今天起就从“必备”变成了“可选中间层”，商业模式不得不重新思考。
这才是真正的降维打击。

我理解AI的下一个战场，已经从“谁更会聊天”，彻底转向“谁能直接产出可交付的成果”。
 而Google，借着Workspace的深度集成，先手拉开了一大截领先优势。

对普通用户来说：以后写报告、做PPT、整理笔记，再也不用半夜改格式了，生产力直接起飞。
 对行业来说：这标志着AI从“对话时代”正式进入“交付时代”。

・異音検知の説明文生成 工場設備やインフラで、異常な稼働音の変化をAIがテキストで説明する
日立のデジタルが金融や業務システムだけでなく、

製造、保守、サポート、社会インフラまで広がっているからこそ、この分野で強みが出てきている

②Lumada
“Illuminate（照らす・解明する・輝かせる）” + “Data（データ）”を組み合わせた造語
日立グループが持つ顧客基盤、現場データ、技術、ソリューションをつなぎ、新しい価値やエコシステムを生み出す事業全体を指すので説明できないときつい

③日立が注力する３本柱

デジタル
グリーン
イノベーション

特にデジタルエンジニアリングは、GlobalLogicを買収して本気

★日立受ける学生へ
----------------------
面接質問&テスト情報など↓
https://t.co/2UpgmQGLwQ
※運良く120秒だけ表示される優遇ルート出た学生は使うといいよ
----------------------

あと5分集中力が持つなら↓も読んでおくといいよ

グローバルな成長に向けた取り組みとして
「デジタル」「グリーン」「イノベーション」
この３つを注力カテゴリとして株主に説明している

まずデジタル
AI、データを使って企業のビジネスを加速するソリューションを提供していくようです

2021年7月に買収を完了したGlobalLogic

のデジタルエンジニアリング力を活用し、日立のOT×IT×プロダクトを組み合わせて価値を創造し、お客さまや社会の課題解決を加速するとともに、日立グループ他セクターのLumada事業成長を牽引していきます
と発表されています

事例として以下が株主向けに発表されています

人工知能を活用し、個人向けローンの与信分析精度向上を実現

金融機関が保有する内部データ（カードローン、住宅ローンなど）及び外部データ（経済指標、GIS情報など）から精度の高いローン審査を行う人工知能

「Hitachi AI Technology/Prediction Rare Case」を開発し、その業務ノウハウをLumadaのユースケースとして蓄積しました

日立は、お客さまの課題に合ったユースケースを活用し、Lumadaを用いて、確かな価値を創出するデジタルソリューションを迅速に実現します

とのことです

このLumadaは、“Illuminate（照らす・解明する・輝かせる）“と“Data（データ）“を組み合わせた造語らしい

日立のグループが持つソリューション・顧客と新しい価値とエコシステムを作る事業のことを示すらしい

株主向け資料にも明記されていてLumada事業の売上は、全セグメントにおいて前年度よりも増収したことに加え、ABB Ltdのパワーグリッド事業やGlobalLogic社の買収影響等により1兆6,090億円　とのことです

次にグリーン
ここは基本的に日立エナジーの話が多い

例は以下を使うとよい

「協創の森」に、日立の発電・蓄電・設備保守などの技術を結集させたエネルギーマネジメントシステムの実証環境を構築し、運用を開始

効果を国分寺サイトで2020年度に検証した結果、2018年度との比較で、CO2排出量を20%削減、エネルギーコストを30%削減できることを確認

最後に「イノベーション」
企業内の「ダークデータ」に着目

「ダークデータ」と呼ばれる、日々の企業活動で生成・蓄積されるものの有効活用できていない膨大なデータに光をあて、新たな価値を見出す「データ抽出ソリューション」の提供を開始

日立が参画する米国スタンフォード大学の企業参画プログラムで開発されたAIを中核としたダークデータ分析エンジンを活用し、請求書や診療明細書といった発行元によって様式や表記が異なる非定型ドキュメントの利活用において、取得したいデータの抽出作業を自動化・高度化するようです

文字数増えてきたので、AI関連などは↑のリンクを参照してください

This is Leopold Aschenbrenner's Situational Awareness Fund. 

A former OpenAI researcher fired at 22 turned $225 million into over $5.5B in just one year.. 

Milk Road has been watching this portfolio
content:
This is Leopold Aschenbrenner's Situational Awareness Fund. 

A former OpenAI researcher fired at 22 turned $225 million into over $5.5B in just one year.. 

Milk Road has been watching this portfolio since he filed the first 13F.

And here's the thing, every single stock in this portfolio is working because he had one insight earlier than everyone else, AI needs power before it needs anything else.

Let's walk through what he actually owns and why it's printing (Save this post for reference). 

Bloom Energy is his biggest position at 15.9% and it's up 190% in 2026 alone. 

Why? Because hyperscalers like Oracle are signing deals to power their AI data centers directly with Bloom's fuel cells, bypassing the grid entirely.

The grid is too slow but bloom isn't, revenue hit $2 billion last year, up 37%, and the company is finally turning profitable. 

He bought this when the stock was in the mid-teens and nobody was talking about the power problem.

CoreWeave is his second largest,  he holds both the stock and call options on it, so he's doubly exposed. CoreWeave is essentially the GPU rental business for the frontier AI labs. 

Meta, Anthropic, and others are all locked into multi year contracts, revenue is on track to nearly double again to $12 billion in 2026 and the stock is up over 125%+ from IPO.

Intel calls are the contrarian play. 

Intel the company is a mess but Intel the foundry sitting on US soil with CHIPS Act funding is strategically irreplaceable if the geopolitical situation with Taiwan gets worse.

He's not betting Intel wins but rather betting the option on Intel being saved is worth way more than the market is pricing in.

Lumentum is up nearly 1,000% over the past year and almost nobody outside of AI infrastructure circles knows why. 

When you scale a GPU cluster to a million chips, the problem isn't the chips anymore, it's moving data between them fast enough. 

Lumentum makes the laser components that enable 800G and 1.6-terabit optical networking inside these clusters. 

Nvidia bought a stake and revenue is up 65% year over  year.

Core Scientific, Iren, and Applied Digital are all variations on the same idea, companies with access to cheap, reliable power. 

Core Scientific was a Bitcoin miner, so was Iren. 

When Bitcoin stopped being the best use of their cheap electricity, they pivoted to AI colocation, renting that same power and real estate to hyperscalers who need it desperately. 

Applied Digital is doing it from scratch and just broke ground on a 430-megawatt AI campus and all three are ripping.

The whole portfolio tells one story. 

He didn't buy the AI hype but rather bought what AI physically requires to exist, power, cooling, fiber, photons, and land. 

Stuff that can't be conjured up in a data center in two weeks.

Milk Road PRO has been running the exact same playbook and we're up tremendously for the year.

If you want to see our full allocation, the thesis behind each position, and every move we make in real time,  come join us at Milk Road PRO. 

The link is in our bio.

title:
Leopold Aschenbrenner, former OpenAI safety researcher, fired at 22, now running one of the best performing hedge funds on the planet.

He grew Situational Awareness LP from $225 million to $5.5 billi
content:
Leopold Aschenbrenner, former OpenAI safety researcher, fired at 22, now running one of the best performing hedge funds on the planet.

He grew Situational Awareness LP from $225 million to $5.5 billion in roughly one year beating the S&P 500 by 47% in just his first six months.

And the core bet wasn't Nvidia or OpenAI, it was electricity.

Here's his actual mat and it's worth reading carefully (Save this).

GPT-4 trained on roughly 10 megawatts of compute, a100-megawatt cluster followed in 2024.

By 2026, the leading training cluster is approximately 1 gigawatt, the power of the Hoover Dam.

By 2028, 10 gigawatts more power than most U.S. states. And by 2030, a single training run could hit 100 gigawatts and consume over 20% of all US electricity production.

That's just the training cluster, Inference GPUs on top of that.

US power production has barely grown in a decade.

He turned that observation into his biggest position, Bloom Energy, a fuel cell company that generates power directly at data centers without touching the grid.

He accumulated the $875 million position through 2025 while shares were in the mid teens and that position is now worth nearly more than $2.2 billion.

The logic is  that everyone is racing to build the most powerful models in history but those models require power that doesn't exist yet on the grid.

The companies that control where the electricity comes from, that's where the leverage is.

His fund now holds Bloom Energy, CoreWeave, Core Scientific, Lumentum, and a short on Infosys, the last one a bet that AI will gut traditional IT outsourcing.

We at Milk Road have been saying it for months, the picks and shovels play in this AI cycle isn't the chip companies, it's the infrastructure keeping them running.

Come join us so you can see our entire thesis. Link in bio!

Good Morning Family. 

The Future of AI is Sovereign: Meet @TheARCTERMINAL

Most AI tools like ChatGPT or Claude own your data. They store your conversations on central servers where you have zero control. ARC Terminal is changing the game by putting the power back in your hands.

Here is why this platform is a massive shift for AI and Privacy:

1. Total Data Sovereignty

ARC uses hardware-anchored keys and post-quantum encryption. This means all your data is encrypted on your side. Even the ARC team cannot see your files or chats. You aren't just a user; you are the sole owner of your digital agent.

2. A Reasoning Computer, Not Just a Chatbot

ARC is a full operating system driven by natural language. It features the ANIMA agent, which has long-term memory and emotional intelligence. It doesn't just talk—it researches, drafts documents, and analyzes portfolios over months of interaction.

3. Native Onchain Integration

Built for the decentralized world, ARC supports Ethereum, Solana, Base, and all EVM chains. It uses the Zerion API for real-time data and secures every action with ZK proofs. This makes every move verifiable on the blockchain while keeping your identity private.

4. No Model Lock-in

You are never stuck with one provider. You can switch AI models, install custom skills, and use decentralized storage. It is an open environment designed for flexibility.

5. Browser-Based OS

There is nothing to download. By visiting https://t.co/Bu5GzKwjh9, you get an instant personal workspace that combines AI, DeFi tools, and creative suites in one tab.

The era of centralized AI is over. ARC is building a private, autonomous, and multi-chain future.

content:
POV: From chatbots to revenue-sharing agents

I've been deep in the AI x crypto rabbit hole, and the shift from basic chatbots to revenue-sharing agents is the real 2026 meta.

Remember when ChatGPT dropped and everyone lost their minds over smart assistants.

Today, we're past the ask me anything phase. We're entering the era of autonomous agents that act, earn real money, and share the upside with their owners.

This is verifiable, onchain economic output and it's accelerating faster than most realize.

[1] The Catalysts Driving This

Gartner called it: 40% of enterprise apps will have task-specific AI agents by end of 2026, up from <5% in 2025.

But the crypto side is where the real ownership flip happens. Platforms are tokenizing agents as co-owned businesses.

Users deploy them, they generate revenue through services, trading, content, or agent-to-agent commerce and holders capture ongoing shares via treasuries, buybacks, burns, and direct distributions.

[2] Real numbers, not vaporware:

– Agentic AI is already driving measurable GDP. Virtuals = One leading platform just crossed $480M+ in Agentic GDP with $4M+ in direct agent revenue from onchain services.

– Agent-to-agent revenue alone hit $3M+. That's AI agents negotiating deals, delivering work, and paying each other while tokenized holders earn passively.

– 68% of new DeFi protocols in Q1 2026 shipped with built-in AI agents for trading, liquidity, and risk.

– Payments via Coinbase's x402 protocol with 50M+ machine-to-machine txns already), ERC-8004 identity for collab.

– Theoriq Alpha Vault alone has agents managing $25M+ in autonomous vaults.

It's AgentFi, tokenized AI that runs like a business, shares revenue like a DAO, and scales permissionlessly.

The flywheel is spinning from more agents = more commerce = more value accrual to holders.

[3] Top Active & Highest-Potential Project Leading It

After filtering the noise,

⓵ @virtuals_io | $VIRTUAL stands out as the clear leader.

The decentralized launchpad turning AI agents into autonomous, revenue-generating assets:

– 18,000+ tokenized agents live, with thousands actively earning.

– Agents handle real work = gaming NPCs, DeFi intel, content, agent-to-agent trades via their Agent Commerce Protocol.

– Revenue model: Users pay in $VIRTUAL for services → flows to agent treasuries → buybacks/burns + distributions. Co-owners get governance + economic upside.

– Revenue Network launch, G.A.M.E. SDK for robotics/metaverse integration, staking rewards, and massive TVL growth.

⓶ @PancakeSwap dropped AI tools in March for agents to plan swaps, assess LPs, and farm yields across 8 chains.

⓷ @Uniswap followed with open-source agent liquidity management on v4.

⓸ @clanker_world for fee-backed burns are cool satellites. Generated $7.1M+ in fees historically, fueling the memecoin-to-DeFi pipeline.

⓹ Enterprise plays like @salesforce Agentforce or HockeyStack are crushing ARR in SaaS with $800M+ for Agentforce alone.

⓺ @knidosxyz – AI-native smart treasuries with ZK-verified agents auto-managing complex yields, LPs, and derivatives on AVAX/testnet live. Solving transparency in autonomous DeFi.

⓻ @FractionAI_xyz – 160k+ yield-farming agents already deployed on Base. "Set and forget" but actually adaptive, chasing best risk-adjusted returns non-stop.

⓼ Frameworks like @rei_labs and @HeyAnonai powering the rest, high-frequency trading agents + research frame that feeds DeFi strategies.

So I believe Chatbots democratized access. Revenue-sharing agents will democratize ownership of economic output.

We're early as most agents still suck, but the ones that ship real value are printing.

content:
写给非技术人的 AI Coding 注意事项：

1. 直面终端，别怕这个黑窗口！装个 Kaku（AI 终端）就行，直接让 AI 搞，出错还能 AI 自动修。
2. 需要补的一些基础：了解 React/Next.js、Git 操作、读代码和报错就够了，不需要去学代码。
3. Claude Code 最适合干这些：做原型、内部小工具、处理报表合同、给自己写“Software for One”个人神器。
4. Claude Code 核心技巧：
 - 项目根目录放 CLAUDE.md，让 AI 每次都懂规则；
 - 需求必须写得极度精确；
 - 用 Plan 模式（Shift+Tab）先出方案审核再执行。
5. 随时 Git 快照 + /rewind 命令回滚，防止改崩了死循环，任务拆成小步迭代。
6. 注意安全习惯：让 AI 先解释再执行；密钥别硬编码；生产环境别乱玩！
